text.title: risk-assessment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for risk-assessment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Demand risk scored against TAM rather than the validated wedge
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A large TAM with no validated entry wedge is the highest-loss failure mode; anchoring on TAM produces artificially low demand-risk scores.

failure-mode.description: Pricing risk collapsed into one row instead of four sub-rows
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aggregating four distinct failure modes (commoditization, price war, anchor mismatch, WTP collapse) into one row averages them and hides the real exposure.

failure-mode.description: Channel-dependence risk scored Medium when one channel exceeds 40% of pipeline
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any single channel above 40% should be auto-scored High probability unless 3+ years of stable retention evidence exists.

failure-mode.description: Risk register updated only at project kick-off
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale registers are worse than none; they create false security. Re-run at minimum monthly and on every commit to source research files.

failure-mode.description: Agent quotes multi-billion-dollar TAM and scores demand risk Low
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the prompt to score against the validated wedge (named buyer segment, confirmed problem, pipeline evidence), not the TAM ceiling.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses stale competitor lists from training data
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to re-read competitive-analysis.md and verify competitor domains via faion-domain-checker-agent before citing any competitive risk row.

failure-mode.description: Agent mitigation language defaults to "diversify channels", "monitor competitors", "iterate pricing"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ban these phrases; require action verb + named owner + measurable trigger + budget cap. Vague mitigations are never executed.

failure-mode.description: Agent ships "Avoid" response on market risk autonomously (e.g., killing a segment)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Avoid-strategy responses mean killing a segment, geo, or pricing tier — never let an agent execute this unattended. Hard human-in-the-loop gate required.

failure-mode.description: Risk IDs change every agent run (R1..Rn renumbered)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use a stable hash of the risk statement or instruct the agent to diff-merge against the existing register; changing IDs break longitudinal tracking.
